stem:
The table below gives the number of employees at a company whose age at the time of hire falls within the interval listed.

Of those employees who were at least 30 years old when they were hired, what fraction were at least 40 years old?
rationale: Choice D is correct. The number of employees at least 30 years old at hire is 12 + 9 + 6 = 27. Of those, the number at least 40 years old is 9 + 6 = 15. Therefore the fraction is 15/27 (which reduces to 5/9). Choice A is incorrect because 15/32 uses the total of all employees as the denominator instead of conditioning on those at least 30. Choice B is incorrect because 9/27 omits the 6 employees who were 50 or older from the numerator. Choice C is incorrect because 6/15 is the fraction of employees at least 40 years old who were at least 50.
